---
title: "segment_segment_sq_dist"
generated: true
description: "Clamped segment-to-segment squared distance (Ericson)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry`](../nitrix-geometry.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.geometry._mesh_distance`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:segment_segment_sq_dist -->
```python
segment_segment_sq_dist(p1: Float[Array, '*batch 3'], q1: Float[Array, '*batch 3'], p2: Float[Array, '*batch 3'], q2: Float[Array, '*batch 3']) -> Float[Array, '*batch']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:segment_segment_sq_dist -->
Clamped segment-to-segment squared distance (Ericson).

The squared Euclidean distance between the closest points of segment
`[p1, q1]` and segment `[p2, q2]` -- the branchless, vectorised form
of Ericson's `ClosestPtSegmentSegment` (*Real-Time Collision
Detection*, section 5.1.9): minimise
$\|(p_1 + s d_1) - (p_2 + t d_2)\|^2$ over
$(s, t) \in [0, 1]^2$ with the parameters clamped to the unit
square.

The proximity core of an edge-edge mesh self-intersection penalty: two
mesh edges intersect (or nearly so) iff this distance is ~0.  Inputs
broadcast over any leading `*batch` (e.g. a set of candidate edge
pairs), so the per-pair distance is one vectorised call.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:segment_segment_sq_dist -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `p1` | `Float` | `*batch` × `3` | — | Endpoints of the first segment, `(*batch, 3)`. |
| `q1` | `Float` | `*batch` × `3` | — | Endpoints of the first segment, `(*batch, 3)`. |
| `p2` | `Float` | `*batch` × `3` | — | Endpoints of the second segment, `(*batch, 3)`. |
| `q2` | `Float` | `*batch` × `3` | — | Endpoints of the second segment, `(*batch, 3)`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `*batch`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:segment_segment_sq_dist -->
::: {.callout-note}
Squared distance (no `sqrt`) -- it is the cheaper, smoother quantity a
penalty thresholds, and the caller takes the root only if a true length
is needed.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:segment_segment_sq_dist -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*batch` | leading batch axes (variadic) |
| `3` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
